data.status: slated for closure; agreed to remain open at least three more years per new incentive agreement
data.short_description: A Harveys Supermarket at 201 W. 48th St. in Jacksonville was slated for closure but Winn-Dixie agreed to keep it open for at least three more years as part of a proposed economic incentive agreement with Jacksonville City Council. The council had previously deferred a vote after residents warned the closure would create a food desert. The store's lease expires in 2029, and Winn-Dixie committed to using "commercially reasonable efforts" to extend it.
